segments.text: Call now; is there any who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For resentment kills the foolish man, and jealousy kills the simple.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I have seen the foolish taking root, but suddenly I cursed his habitation.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
His children are far from safety. They are crushed in the gate. 
Neither is there any to deliver them,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
whose harvest the hungry eat up, and take it even out of the thorns. 
The snare gapes for their substance.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For affliction doesn’t come out of the dust, neither does trouble spring out of the ground;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: but man is born to trouble, as the sparks fly upward.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “But as for me, I would seek God. I would commit my cause to God,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: who does great things that can’t be fathomed, marvelous things without number;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: who gives rain on the earth, and sends waters on the fields;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: so that he sets up on high those who are low, those who mourn are exalted to safety.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He frustrates the plans of the crafty, so that their hands can’t perform their enterprise.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He takes the wise in their own craftiness; the counsel of the cunning is carried headlong.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They meet with darkness in the day time, and grope at noonday as in the night.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But he saves from the sword of their mouth, even the needy from the hand of the mighty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So the poor has hope, and injustice shuts her mouth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Behold, happy is the man whom God corrects. Therefore do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For he wounds and binds up. He injures and his hands make whole.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He will deliver you in six troubles; yes, in seven no evil will touch you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In famine he will redeem you from death; in war, from the power of the sword.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will be hidden from the scourge of the tongue, neither will you be afraid of destruction when it comes.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will laugh at destruction and famine, neither will you be afraid of the animals of the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For you will be allied with the stones of the field. The animals of the field will be at peace with you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will know that your tent is in peace. You will visit your fold, and will miss nothing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will know also that your offspring will be great, your offspring as the grass of the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will come to your grave in a full age, like a shock of grain comes in its season.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Behold, we have researched it. It is so. Hear it, and know it for your good.
segments.speaker: Unknown
